pattern: The string is short enough to treat every subset of its positions as a candidate subsequence, check which subsets read the same forwards and backwards, and then for every palindromic subset pair it against every palindromic subset built only from the leftover positions.
hints: With at most twelve characters, every one of the 2^n position subsets can be tested directly for whether the characters it picks out, in order, form a palindrome.
The two subsequences can never reuse a position, so once you fix one subset of positions, the other subsequence's positions must come only from what is left over.
For each palindromic subset, walk through the palindromic subsets of its complement (the positions it didn't use) and keep the best product of the two subset sizes seen.

edge_inputs.description: mixed word with a good split
longer word with several repeated letters
one repeated letter at the max length
alternating two-letter pattern at the max length
no repeated letters at all, only single-character palindromes possible

stress.description: random short strings over a small alphabet to encourage repeated letters
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 12))
    alphabet = 'abcde'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))]
